notes: Music & Performing Arts draft — 15 clues grounded in Wikipedia lead extracts. Leans toward under-weight tiers $200 and $1000 per active-bank signal. Subcategory spread: Popular Music (6), Opera (4), Instruments (2), Jazz & Blues (2), Film Scores & Soundtracks (1). Tier spread: $200×2, $400×4, $600×4, $800×2, $1000×3.

questions.clue: This English singer-songwriter, born Adele Laurie Blue Adkins, has sold more than 120 million records and received 16 Grammy Awards, and her 2015 single 'Hello' broke the UK digital download record in its first week.

citations.title: Adele
citations.snippet: Adele Laurie Blue Adkins is an English singer and songwriter. Regarded as a British cultural icon, she is known for her mezzo-soprano vocals and sentimental songwriting. Her accolades include 16 Grammy Awards, 12 Brit Awards, an Academy Awa

questions.clue: The second-largest bowed string instrument in the violin family, this instrument's name is an abbreviation of violoncello; it is played upright between the legs of a seated performer and voices the tenor and bass registers of the string section.

citations.title: Cello
citations.snippet: The violoncello ( VY-ə-lən-CHEL-oh, Italian pronunciation: [vjolonˈtʃɛllo]), commonly abbreviated as cello ( CHEL-oh), is a medium-low pitched bowed string instrument of the violin family. Its four strings are usually tuned in perfect fifth

questions.clue: With 27 Grammy Awards and 5 Academy Awards to his name, this composer became synonymous with blockbuster film scoring through his decades-long collaborations with Steven Spielberg and George Lucas.

citations.title: John Williams
citations.snippet: John Towner Williams is an American composer and conductor. Over his seven-decade career, he has composed many of the best known scores in film history. His style blends romanticism, impressionism and atonal music with complex orchestration

questions.clue: Originating in Naples in the early 18th century as a contrast to opera seria, this genre of Italian comic opera reached its zenith in such works as Rossini's 'The Barber of Seville' and Mozart's 'The Marriage of Figaro.'

citations.title: Opera buffa
citations.snippet: Opera buffa is a genre of opera. It was first used as an informal description of Italian comic operas variously classified by their authors as commedia in musica, commedia per musica, dramma bernesco, dramma comico, divertimento giocoso.

questions.clue: Formed in London in 1964 with Roger Daltrey, Pete Townshend, and Keith Moon, this rock band pioneered the concept of the rock opera with Tommy (1969) and Quadrophenia (1973).

citations.title: The Who
citations.snippet: The Who are an English rock band formed in London in 1964. Their classic lineup (1964–1978) consisted of lead vocalist Roger Daltrey, guitarist Pete Townshend, bassist John Entwistle, and drummer Keith Moon. Considered one of the most influ

questions.clue: Opening with the exuberant nonsense phrase 'A-wop-bop-a-loo-bop-a-wop-bam-boom,' this 1955 Little Richard recording was one of the first rock and roll records to cross over from the R&B charts to the mainstream pop charts.

citations.title: Tutti Frutti (song)
citations.snippet: "Tutti Frutti" is a song written by Little Richard and Dorothy LaBostrie, recorded in 1955, which was Richard's first major hit. With its energetic refrain and its hard-driving sound and wild lyrics, it became not only a model for many futu

questions.clue: This Brazilian genre emerged in the late 1950s by blending the rhythms of samba with the harmonics of cool jazz; closely associated with João Gilberto and later popularized internationally by 'The Girl from Ipanema.'

citations.title: Bossa nova
citations.snippet: Bossa nova is a relaxed style of samba developed in the late 1950s and early 1960s in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. It is mainly characterized by a calm syncopated rhythm with chords and fingerstyle mimicking the beat of a samba groove, as if it

questions.clue: A self-contained piece for solo voice—with or without instrumental accompaniment—this operatic form takes its name from the Italian word for 'air' and commonly appears within operas, oratorios, and cantatas to allow a singer to express a character's inner state.

citations.title: Aria
citations.snippet: In music, an aria is a self-contained piece for one voice, with or without instrumental or orchestral accompaniment, normally part of a larger work.

questions.clue: Unlike the piano—which strikes its strings with felt hammers—this earlier keyboard instrument produces sound by plucking its strings with quills or plastic plectra mounted on wooden jacks; it dominated Western keyboard music before the piano's rise in the 18th century.

citations.title: Harpsichord
citations.snippet: A harpsichord is a keyboard instrument that makes its sound by plucking a set of strings. In a harpsichord, depressing a key raises its back end within the instrument, which in turn lifts one or more jacks, each a thin strip of wood holding

questions.clue: Regarded as the dominant figure in Italian opera following the retirements of Rossini, Donizetti, and Bellini, this 19th-century composer's works—including 'La traviata' and 'Rigoletto'—remained anchored to vocal melody and dramatic expression rather than Wagnerian orchestral philosophy.

citations.title: Giuseppe Verdi
citations.snippet: Giuseppe Fortunino Francesco Verdi was an Italian composer best known for his operas. He was born near Busseto, a small town in the modern province of Parma, to a family of moderate means, receiving a musical education with the help of a lo

questions.clue: No other jazz musician steered the genre through as many stylistic revolutions: this trumpeter was central to the development of bebop, cool jazz, hard bop, modal jazz, and jazz fusion over a career spanning five decades.

citations.title: Miles Davis
citations.snippet: Miles Dewey Davis III was an American trumpeter, bandleader and composer. He is among the most influential and acclaimed figures in the history of jazz and 20th-century music. In a career spanning nearly five decades, Davis was at the foref

questions.clue: Born O'Shea Jackson in Los Angeles, this rapper formed his first group, C.I.A., in 1986 before co-founding N.W.A. with Eazy-E and Dr. Dre in 1987; he was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame as an N.W.A. member in 2016.

citations.title: Ice Cube
citations.snippet: O'Shea Jackson, known professionally as Ice Cube, is an American rapper, songwriter, actor, and filmmaker. His efforts on N.W.A's 1989 album Straight Outta Compton contributed to gangsta rap's popularity, and his political rap solo albums A

topic_entities: rock and roll hall of fame

questions.clue: Signed to Eazy-E's Ruthless Records after travelling from Cleveland to Los Angeles to audition, this hip-hop group originated a melodic 'chopper' flow that bridged gangsta rap and R&B; their 1995 single 'Tha Crossroads' won a Grammy for Best Rap Performance by a Duo or Group.

citations.title: Bone Thugs-n-Harmony
citations.snippet: Bone Thugs-n-Harmony is an American hip-hop group formed in 1991 in Cleveland, Ohio. Consisting of rappers Bizzy Bone, Wish Bone, Layzie Bone, Krayzie Bone, and Flesh-n-Bone, Bone Thugs-n-Harmony are known for helping pioneer the chopper ra

questions.clue: This British singer-songwriter won the Grammy Award for Best New Artist in 2025 after the release of her debut album 'Messy'; her sound fuses neo-soul, jazz, and R&B influences cultivated partly during her studies at the BRIT School.

citations.title: Olivia Dean
citations.snippet: Olivia Lauryn Dean is an English singer and songwriter. Her accolades include four Brit Awards and the Grammy Award for Best New Artist.

questions.clue: Labelled by Wagner a 'Bühnenfestspiel für drei Tage und einen Vorabend' (a stage festival play for three days and a preliminary evening), this four-opera cycle based on Norse and Germanic mythology takes approximately fifteen hours to perform in its entirety.

citations.title: Der Ring des Nibelungen
